text:
If you like it dry, sunny, and calm, today's your day, this stretch of easy weather doesn't last past tonight. Expect a genuinely pleasant Monday, sunny with a high near 83, light southwest wind, and humidity that's staying reasonable rather than tropical. Air quality's good, so no real caveats there. Out on the lake it's about as gentle as Champlain gets, winds under 10 knots and waves a foot or less, with water sitting around 70. That calm holds into this evening before south winds start building overnight, so if you want a smooth paddle or sail, get it in before dark.

Tuesday is the flip. Showers arrive around midday and the better chance of thunderstorms builds in through the afternoon, with a low-end severe risk mainly for gusty wind and locally heavy rain. Highs only reach the high 70s to low 80s depending on which model you trust, and the wind really cranks on the lake, gusting past 30 knots with waves building to 3 or 4 feet by afternoon. If you're boating Tuesday, plan to be off the water well before midday. Tonight, though, just enjoy it: comfortable, clear-ish, good for a walk or a late dinner outside.
packet:
OBSERVED (2026-07-20T11:45:00+00:00): Clear, 64F feels 64F, humidity 64%, wind S 7 mph
NWS FORECAST:
  Today: Sunny, with a high near 83. Southwest wind around 6 mph.
  Tonight: Mostly cloudy, with a low around 65. South wind 5 to 14 mph.
  Tuesday: A chance of rain showers between noon and 2pm, then showers and thunderstorms likely between 2pm and 4pm, then a chance of showers and thunderstorms. Partly sunny, with a high near 82. South wind 15 to 18 mph, with gusts as high as 32 mph. Chance of precipitation is 60%. New rainfall amounts less than a tenth of an inch possible.
  Tuesday Night: Showers and thunderstorms. Mostly cloudy, with a low around 65. South wind 10 to 16 mph, with gusts as high as 31 mph. Chance of precipitation is 80%. New rainfall amounts between a tenth and quarter of an inch possible.
AFD KEY MESSAGES (forecaster's reasoning, issued 2026-07-20T11:12:00+00:00):
  1. Dry through today then showers and some thunderstorms Tuesday into Wednesday.
  2. Near normal temperatures expected for the second half of the week into the weekend. No significant weather anticipated at this time.
AFD WHAT CHANGED: As of 257 AM EDT Monday... No significant changes.
LAKE (broad waters):
  Today: Southwest winds 10 knots or less, becoming south. Sunny. Waves 1 foot or less.
  Tonight: South winds 5 to 15 knots, increasing to 15 to 20 knots. Partly cloudy in the evening, then becoming mostly cloudy. Waves 1 to 2 feet, building to 2 to 4 feet.
  Tuesday: South winds to 30 knots. Partly sunny. A chance of showers and thunderstorms in the afternoon. Waves 2 to 4 feet, subsiding to 1 to 3 feet.
  Tuesday Night: South winds 15 to 25 knots. Showers in the evening, then showers with a chance of thunderstorms overnight. Waves 1 foot or less.
LAKE GAGE: water 70F, level 95.95 ft (normal)
MODEL SPREAD (where forecasts diverge):
  2026-07-20: highs [GFS 82, Euro 81, ICON 80] spread 2F; precip chance [GFS 1%, Euro 6%, ICON 0%]
  2026-07-21: highs [GFS 80, Euro 77, ICON 78] spread 3F; precip chance [GFS 80%, Euro 80%, ICON 64%]
  2026-07-22: highs [GFS 73, Euro 82, ICON 76] spread 9F; precip chance [GFS 80%, Euro 77%, ICON 60%]
AIR: AQI 26 Good (PM2.5) — On Monday, July 20th, expect generally good air quality across Vermont.
SUN: rise 2026-07-20T05:27-04:00, set 2026-07-20T20:31-04:00, UV max 7.35
WEATHER UNDERGROUND / WEATHER.COM:
  high 83, low 67: More sun than clouds. Highs in the low 80s and lows in the upper 60s.
  high 79, low 68: Afternoon showers and thunderstorms. Highs in the upper 70s and lows in the upper 60s.
WCAX METEOROLOGIST: Tuesday we start cloudy, with the chance for a few spotty rain showers earlier in the day, but we’ll see the better chance of showers and thunderstorms heading into Tuesday afternoon. Right now, the bulk of our area is under a Marginal Risk for severe weather — Level 1 on a scale of 1 to 5. A stronger storm could produce gusty winds and locally heavy rain. Otherwise, just general summertime thunderstorm activity can be expected. High temperatures for the day climb back into the 70s. We may see some smoke return as winds shift to being out of the south, but it shouldn’t be nearly as bad as what the bulk of the area saw Saturday.
